description: Length-matched V2 facts for number theory conjectures domain.

facts.truth: every even integer greater than 2 is the sum of two primes
distractors: every odd integer greater than 5 is the sum of two primes
every even integer above 2 equals the product of two primes
every integer greater than 1 is the difference of two primes

facts.context: The weak Goldbach conjecture (odd Goldbach) states every odd n>5 is sum of three primes. Its status is:
distractors: proven by Terence Tao in 2012
refuted by a counterexample at n=77
reduced to the Riemann hypothesis

facts.context: The Twin Prime Conjecture states that:
facts.truth: there are infinitely many pairs of primes differing by 2
distractors: there are infinitely many pairs of primes differing by 4
every prime above 5 has a twin prime within distance 2
there are infinitely many pairs of primes differing by 6

facts.context: Zhang's 2013 breakthrough on bounded gaps between primes showed:
facts.truth: infinitely many prime pairs with gap at most 70 million, now reduced to 246
distractors: infinitely many prime pairs with gap at most 2 million, now reduced to 12
infinitely many prime pairs with gap at most 600, fully resolving the conjecture
finitely many prime pairs exist with bounded gaps, contradicting earlier heuristics

facts.context: The Collatz Conjecture states that iterating n->n/2 (even) or n->3n+1 (odd):
facts.truth: eventually reaches 1 for every positive integer
distractors: eventually reaches 1 only for odd positive integers
eventually diverges to infinity for some integers
eventually reaches 1 or enters a cycle above 4

facts.context: Computational verification of the Collatz Conjecture has confirmed it for:
distractors: all integers up to approximately 10^12
all integers up to approximately 10^30
all integers up to approximately 10^15

facts.context: The ABC Conjecture relates the prime factors of a, b, and a+b=c. It states:
facts.truth: for any e>0, finitely many triples satisfy c > rad(abc)^(1+e)
distractors: for any e>0, infinitely many triples satisfy c > rad(abc)^(1+e)
for any e>0, finitely many triples satisfy c < rad(abc)^(1-e)
for any e>0, finitely many triples satisfy c > rad(abc)^(2+e)

facts.context: Mochizuki's claimed proof of ABC using Inter-universal Teichmuller Theory (IUT):
facts.truth: remains controversial with key steps disputed by Scholze and Stix
distractors: was fully validated and accepted by the mathematical community in 2021
was formally withdrawn by Mochizuki after errors found by Scholze
remains unreviewed since no referee has examined the full manuscript

facts.context: Legendre's Conjecture states that between n^2 and (n+1)^2 there is:
facts.truth: always at least one prime number (unproven)
distractors: always at least two prime numbers (proven)
sometimes no prime at all for large enough n
always exactly one prime number (unproven)

facts.context: A Sophie Germain prime p satisfies that 2p+1 is also prime. The conjecture states:
facts.truth: infinitely many Sophie Germain primes exist (unproven)
distractors: finitely many Sophie Germain primes exist (unproven)
infinitely many Sophie Germain primes exist (proven)
all primes below 10^8 are Sophie Germain primes

facts.context: Fermat numbers F_n = 2^(2^n) + 1 are prime for n=0,1,2,3,4. The open question is:
facts.truth: whether infinitely many Fermat primes exist; none found for n>4
distractors: whether finitely many Fermat primes exist; one found at n=7
whether all Fermat numbers are composite; proven true for n>4
whether infinitely many Fermat numbers are composite; none for n<5

facts.context: Mersenne primes have form 2^p - 1 where p is prime. It is conjectured that:
facts.truth: infinitely many Mersenne primes exist (unproven, 51 known)
distractors: finitely many Mersenne primes exist (unproven, 51 known)
infinitely many Mersenne primes exist (proven by Lenstra)
all numbers of form 2^p - 1 are prime when p is prime
